sections.title: Before speaking to the Easter Bunny
Talking to Wheeler about cheering up the Easter Bunny

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Can I interest you in a drink?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now, thank you. I was wondering if you might be able to help me with something else.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: And what would that be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know the Easter Bunny?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: I do! He's just over there. Lovely guy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does he seem unhappy to you?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Now that you mention it, yes he does.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: He was always so cheery, bouncing around like a fuzzy avatar of joy.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: He's been in a sorry state lately though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed, I've never seen him like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you noticed anything odd?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hmm... He used to drink pint after pint of carrot juice! Barely sips the stuff now though, just stares into the glass.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: He used to LOVE it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting, could I try the carrot juice please?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Absolutely! One C.J. coming right up!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Euargh! That's...

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Good right? I make it myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's definitely an experience, how do you make it?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: I put some carrots into a juice press, then add some good old-fashioned elbow grease.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would explain the flavour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any particularly nice carrots we could use to make some special juice just for the Easter Bunny?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think he would perhaps appreciate something different.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: You can ask Weasel in the kitchen, he chooses which carrots get used for juice.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Be warned though, he's not much of a people person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by that?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Oh you'll soon find out! Don't worry though, just tell him I sent you and that you're trying to help the Easter Bunny.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: He'll give you some fantastic carrots.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Once you have them, you can use the juice press to extract their goodness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon!

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler again without going to Cooky Weasel

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Have you been to see Weasel in the kitchen about those nice carrots yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Well there's no need to be scared of him! Fetch some carrots and I'll let you use my juice press.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not scared, I just haven't gotten round to it yet.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Whatever you say.

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler after getting the carrots

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: How's the juicing going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, I lost the glass.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Lost or broke? I'm not stupid. Those glasses are expensive you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, can I have another glass please?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Make some room for a new one and you can, yes.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Yes you can, but take care of it this time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, and I will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm about to get juicing!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Well hurry up, we don't want to keep the Easter Bunny waiting!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Remember, the juice press is in the storage area behind the bar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I remember!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Then why are you still here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good question, I'll go and make some juice.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need more carrots..

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Why? What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's call it a quality control incident.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: You drank the juice didn't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Right well you best go see Weasel again, but he won't be happy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is he ever happy?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hah! Good point! Best hurry along now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Ooh look at those carrots, fantastic! This juice is sure to be a treat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's hope so.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: You'll need a special glass for the juice, come back when you have space for one.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Here's a carrot juice glass, make sure you use this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't this just a beer glass?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Ha! Just a beer glass! You're a real joker!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: It's VERY important that you use the correct glassware.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I will. Goodness me.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: The juice press is in the storage room behind the bar. Have fun, and don't make too much mess!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do, thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler while holding fancy carrot juice

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Ohhhh! Look at that juice! Such colour. It smells divine too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh it's good stuff, I should know.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Well let's see what the Easter Bunny thinks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and give it to him now, bye!

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler after giving the juice

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hi [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think that fancy carrot juice has cheered the Easter Bunny up a bit, he really seemed to like it!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: That's great!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Any time.

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler after reading the recipe
Talking to Wheeler while holding the spices

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: I bet those spices will bring those buns to life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Judging by the smell, I'd say so. Powerful stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm making some Easter Buns for the Easter Bunny, and I need some Easter Spices.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any I could use?

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Oh yes of course, we keep plenty behind the bar for cocktails.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Of course I can't give you the spices unless you can carry them. Come back when you have space!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: There you go, I hope they work well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Wheeler with progress in both carrot juice and buns

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

sections.title: Wheeler after juice and bun are done

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for both juice and spices, EB really liked the buns!

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Hi there, nice to see you! Care for a mug of beer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm a bit busy at the moment, maybe later.

lines.speaker: Wheeler
lines.text: Well alright then.
